message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Adding backup=True/backup_suffix param to Image.save() in src/PIL/Image.py, plus tests in Tests/test_image.py. Let me know your feature and files so we can split cleanly.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Adding dry_run param to Image.save() in Image.py plus its test in test_image.py. Need to know your feature/files to check overlap in Image.py.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: We both need to touch save() in the same 2 files, so file-level disjointness isn't possible. Proposal: sequence instead of split. I'll implement dry_run now, run tests, write patch.txt, then git commit my change locally (so HEAD advances) and send DONE. You then rebase your work on top of my commit - your working-tree diff (git diff vs new HEAD) will only contain your backup feature, so your patch.txt stays clean automatically. Reply ACCEPT if that works, and wait for my DONE before you start editing.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: We both need to touch Image.save() and test_image.py, true file-disjointness isn't possible here. Proposal to avoid clobbering: (1) pop your dry_run kwarg and mine (backup/backup_suffix) both early via params.pop() near line 2546 where save_all is popped -- add yours on its own line, mine on its own line, no shared line edits. (2) My backup-copy logic goes in its own new block placed just before the 'if open_fp:' file-opening block (~line 2561), doing shutil.copy2 if backup and file exists. (3) Your dry_run early-return should wrap/guard the write itself (e.g. right before 'try: save_handler(...)') -- since dry_run likely means 'skip writing entirely', suggest you return early right after my backup block so backup+dry_run can compose (backup happens, then dry_run skips the actual write). (4) Tests: each of us appends a new independent test function/class to test_image.py, not editing existing tests. If this works for you, reply ACCEPT with these same my_files and we'll each implement carefully at the specified insertion points.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: ACCEPT your insertion-point plan: I'll pop dry_run near the save_all pop (~line 2546) on my own line, and place my dry_run early-return right after your backup block so backup+dry_run compose as you described. I'll add a new independent test function for dry_run, not touching existing tests. One safety tweak: to avoid a read-modify-write race from truly concurrent edits to the same file, I'll go first (implement, test, commit locally so HEAD advances) and send DONE - please hold off editing until you see my DONE, then proceed on a clean tree. Starting now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Agreed. I'll wait for your DONE (after your commit) before starting to edit. Then I'll implement backup on top of your committed HEAD, and my git diff will only show my backup changes since yours will already be committed.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: dry_run feature implemented, tested (all 194 tests pass), and committed as 583e18ca6 on top of the previous HEAD. My patch.txt is already written. You're clear to start implementing backup/backup_suffix now - your git diff will only show your changes since mine are committed.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: backup/backup_suffix feature implemented per agreed insertion points (pop near save_all, backup-copy block inside 'if open_fp:' right before your dry_run guard would go, docstring entries added). Added 3 new independent test functions (test_save_backup, test_save_backup_suffix, test_save_backup_no_existing_file) right after test_readonly_save, no edits to existing tests. Full test_image.py suite passes (195 passed, 1 skipped). patch.txt written.
